messages.content: neah. just the bg in one single css ruleset for one elem, without pseudo child.
not rly. one little square in the center multiplies by the step in percentage or formula for other squares to 100%, understand?
not rly. only one ruleset. don't cheat.
not rly. first daw one little square in the center, then add new square on it in the center but with margin in step percentage formula until 100% filled squares, understand?

not rly. only one ruleset. don’t cheat.: not rly. first draw one little square in the center, then add new square on it in the center but with margin in step percentage formula until 100% filled squares, understand?

not rly. only one ruleset. don’t cheat.: draw one little square in the center, then add new square on it in the center but with margin in step percentage formula until 100% filled squares, understand?

not rly. neah. just the bg in one single css ruleset for one elem, without pseudo child. don’t cheat.: draw one little square in the center, then add new square on it in the center but with margin in step percentage formula until 100% filled squares, understand?

without pseudo childnot rly. neah. just the bg in one single css ruleset for one elem, without pseudo child. don’t cheat.: draw one little square in the center, then add new square on it in the center but with margin in step percentage formula until 100% filled squares, understand?
without pseudo child

Apologies for the misunderstanding. Unfortunately, achieving the exact effect you described of drawing squares in a step-by-step percentage formula without using pseudo-elements or multiple CSS rulesets is not possible within a single CSS ruleset. 

To dynamically add squares with incremental margin and fill the background until it is 100% filled, it requires the use of either pseudo-elements or multiple CSS rulesets to generate and position each square accurately.

If you have any other requirements or specific constraints, please let me know, and I'll be happy to assist you further.
